sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.32 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: A group word is said to be universal on a group\/$G$if its values on$G$run over the whole of$G$. For an arbitrary constant$c > 8/5$, J.\,L.\,Brenner, R.\,J.\,Evans, and D.\,M.\,Silberger (Proc. Amer. Math. Soc., 96, no.\,1 (1986), 23--28) proved that there exists a number$N_0=N_0(c)$such that the word$x^ry^s$,$rs 0$, is universal on the alternating group${ A}_n$for any$n \, \{ N_0, \; \, c \, m(r,...
